title: Two-minute map-driven mini-episode end to end
short_description: Author a 2-minute map-driven mini-episode in crude (borders shift, arrows advance, labels pop, camera flies) that verifies and renders end-to-end — the map engine's integration proof.
long_description_md:
# Two-minute map-driven mini-episode end to end

## Why task exists

Every other Phase 4 task builds one slice (data/projections, node types, camera state, verify rules) in isolation with unit and fixture-level tests. None of them, alone, proves the map engine actually works as a whole for a real authored project the way an agent will actually use it -- write a script, run verify, fix, render. ROADMAP Phase 4's final done-when bullet exists precisely to force that integration proof before the phase is called done: "A 2-minute map-driven mini-episode (borders shift, arrows advance, labels pop, camera flies) verifies and renders end-to-end in crude." This is the same pattern Phase 1 used ("a scripted 60-90s multi-scene project renders to a correct 1080p30 MP4") and Phase 6 repeats at full length (15-20 min episode) -- a working end-to-end artifact, not just green unit tests, is the actual bar. Skipping this and calling Phase 4 done on unit tests alone would be exactly the kind of quietly-narrowed scope COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md forbids: node types and verify rules that pass isolated fixtures but were never actually exercised together in one authored project the way an agent will use them.

## Roadmap fit

ROADMAP Phase 4: Map engine, final done-when bullet, verbatim scope. Depends on map-camera-state (need real camera flight -- center/zoom paths -- to author "camera flies") and map-verify-rules (need MAP.* checks live and passing to prove "verifies... end-to-end" means something, not just that verify ran without crashing). Transitively exercises map-geometry-foundation (territory index + projections + simplification) and map-node-types (BaseMap, Territory, Border, InvasionArrow, MapLabel, MapMarker) plus everything crude style pack and the Phase 1/2 render+verify pipeline already shipped -- this task authors real content against all of it, does not rebuild any of it.

## Scope

### 1. Author the fixture project

A real ~2-minute (roughly 24-40 beats at typical WPM pacing, exact beat count whatever the authored narration naturally produces) multi-scene project under the repo's fixture/cookbook path, using project.py + beat-annotated script.md per the standard anima new shape, pinned to the crude style pack. Content must exercise, on screen, all four Phase 4 done-when behaviors named in the roadmap bullet:

- **Borders shift**: at least one Territory/Border node whose dataset-ref date or fill changes across beats (a historical border change, or a territory fill/highlight toggling as narration moves on) -- exercises map-state persistence (fills/labels/camera carry over unless changed, per data-model.md) and MAP.DATE_MISMATCH's passing path.
- **Arrows advance**: at least two InvasionArrow nodes with curved growth animation across their visible windows, landing inside the camera viewport at every sampled time -- exercises MAP.ARROW_OUT_OF_VIEW's passing path.
- **Labels pop**: MapLabel nodes entering with the pack's label-pop animation, timed to beats.
- **Camera flies**: at least one multi-step camera flight (center/zoom path spanning at least a world-to-regional zoom, not just a static viewport) using map-camera-state's viewport API.

All dataset-ref territory ids, projections, and dates used must be real, valid entries from map-geometry-foundation's vendored index -- no synthetic/mock territory data in this fixture, since the whole point is exercising the real data path.

### 2. Clean verify pass

`anima verify --json` against the authored project returns pass=true (zero error-severity findings) -- not merely "ran without an exception," but a report snapshot-tested against a checked-in fixture VerificationReport, confirming structural checks, layout/readability checks, and all four map-verify-rules checks (map-verify-rules) were actually evaluated against real map content and came back clean, not skipped. If any check family never fires against this project (e.g. because no node happens to trigger it), that is treated as a coverage gap in the fixture content, not a passing result -- the project's content must be rich enough that every relevant map check family has something real to check.

### 3. Render and determinism proof at map-engine scale

`anima render` against the same project produces a 1080p30 MP4 whose actual duration matches the compiled timeline's ~2-minute total within the beat-duration tolerance the timeline compiler already guarantees, inspected via PyAV/ffprobe (resolution, frame count, duration), not eyeballed. Two independent renders of the same project+seed produce byte-identical frame hashes end to end -- the Phase 1 determinism proof ("same project+seed renders byte-identical frame hashes across two runs") re-run at map-engine scale, since map rendering (projection math, topology-simplified geometry, camera viewport math) is exactly the kind of new numerical surface that could silently reintroduce nondeterminism the earlier proof never exercised.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Any new node type, verify rule, camera capability, or data-loading behavior -- this task authors content against surfaces already shipped by map-geometry-foundation, map-node-types, map-camera-state, map-verify-rules; if authoring this project surfaces a real bug or missing capability in any of those, that is a defect report against the relevant task/LEARNINGS.md entry, not scope for this task to silently patch around.
- Founder style checkpoint / "looks unmistakably X" subjective sign-off -- that pattern exists explicitly in Phase 3 (crude pack) and Phase 5 (flat pack) as a separate logged step; this task's bar is deterministic verify+render success, not a taste review.
- The Phase 7 cookbook entry (a polished, documented, CI-rendered worked example agents pattern-match from) -- that is a separate Phase 7 task that may reuse or supersede this fixture project, not build it for the first time here.
- The full 15-20 minute flagship episode and its nightly-CI/timing-regression gate -- Phase 6, a different scale and a different task.
- flat-pack rendering of this content -- crude only, per the roadmap bullet; flat-pack pluggability proof is Phase 5 scope on its own fixture.
- Subtitle rendering/verification -- already proven in Phase 2's fixture, not re-exercised here unless naturally present in the authored narration; not a required behavior for this task's done condition.

## Acceptance shape

Done means: one authored project, checked into the fixture/cookbook path, whose narration and map content genuinely exercise borders shifting, arrows advancing, labels popping, and a camera flight in crude; `anima verify --json` on it returns a clean, snapshot-tested pass covering every relevant structural, layout, and map-specific check family; `anima render` on it produces a duration/resolution-correct MP4 and reproduces byte-identical frame hashes across two independent runs.

deliverables.description: Author the ~2-minute crude-pack fixture project (project.py + beat-annotated script.md, standard anima new shape) under the repo's fixture/cookbook path, using only real vendored dataset-ref territory ids/dates/projections and exercising, on screen: a border/territory-fill or date change across beats, at least two InvasionArrow growth animations landing inside the camera viewport, MapLabel entries with the pack's label-pop animation, and at least one multi-step world-to-regional camera flight. A build-time test confirms the project constructs a valid scene graph and its compiled timeline totals within tolerance of ~2 minutes, and that the node inventory actually contains at least one of each required map node type and animation family (no silently-thin fixture).

anima verify --json against the authored project returns pass=true with zero error-severity findings, snapshot-tested against a checked-in VerificationReport fixture, and the test asserts every relevant check family (structural, layout/readability, and all four map-verify-rules MAP.* checks) actually produced evaluation coverage against this project's real content rather than trivially finding nothing to check.

anima render against the same project produces a 1080p30 MP4 whose duration/frame count/resolution (inspected via PyAV/ffprobe, not eyeballed) match the compiled timeline's ~2-minute total within the timeline compiler's existing tolerance, and two independent renders of the same project+seed produce byte-identical frame hashes end to end, re-proving Phase 1's determinism guarantee at map-engine numerical scale (projection math, simplified geometry, camera viewport math).
